tldr.text: SPY -1.21%, QQQ -1.51%, SOXX -3.92%, XLK -1.76%, while IGV +1.26% held up better on Friday May 15, 2026; broad risk-off into the weekend hit semis/hardware hardest, while software and a few idiosyncratic winners outperformed.
MU -6.83% as memory names sold off hard on DRAM/HBM de-risking despite no new filing.
AXTI +12.33% bucked the semiconductor selloff on AI/indium phosphide demand and a $632.5M equity raise.
WOLF -10.93% led power/WBG losers on SiC/EV de-risking and balance-sheet worries.
GSIT -9.42% and other speculative memory/IP names were hit on positioning and thin liquidity.
MSFT +3.63% and broader software (TEAM, NOW, CRM, ADBE, INTU, SNOW, DDOG, ZS) showed strong relative strength versus hardware and semis.
QCOM +2.03% and RF names (SWKS, QRVO, MXL) reversed weakness and outperformed into the close.

bullets.text: Software broadly outperformed with IGV +1.26% on a weak tech tape, reinforcing a rotation toward software and AI monetization over hardware beta.
TEAM +7.39% was the standout leader in collaboration/dev tools, consistent with an earnings/guide-style rerating.
NOW +5.02%, CRM +4.05%, ADBE +4.77%, and INTU +4.14% reflected strong enterprise and creative/document software demand.
SNOW +4.19% and DDOG +3.38% showed continued strength in data and observability software.
ZS +4.36% highlighted ongoing bid for cybersecurity software.
MSFT +3.63% advanced despite broad tech weakness, underscoring perceived strength of its AI/software monetization.
ORCL -0.91% lagged broader software even as enterprise peers were strong, with no fresh company-specific catalyst cited.
SOXX -3.92% as semiconductors and hardware led downside in a broad risk-off session.
MU -6.83% was the clearest HBM/memory stress point as memory names sold off on de-risking without new filings.
GSIT -9.42% and MRAM -9.02% saw sharp selling in speculative memory/IP tied to AI and micro-cap positioning.
RMBS -2.75% gave back some gains as memory IP weakened.
ASX -3.46% and AMKR -2.69% reflected pressure in advanced packaging/OSAT, while TSEM -1.08% and UMC +0.03% showed relatively better performance in mature-node foundry.
NVDA -3.60%, AMD -4.45%, AVGO -3.07%, TSM -3.11%, and ARM -6.19% indicated broad de-risking in AI compute and design beta after earlier strength.
INTC -5.71% traded weak on foundry skepticism with pre-market downside carrying through to the close.
QCOM +2.03% stood out with relative strength versus the red semi tape after reversing early weakness.
AI infrastructure proxies were weak as investors de-risked hyperscaler and data center supply chains.
SMCI -5.08% reflected concerns over AI server builders tied to hyperscaler vendor lists and margin worries.
ANET -2.86% and VRT -1.98% sold off as AI data-center networking and power names were de-risked.
AMZN -1.64% was pressured by its hyperscaler capex exposure, while MSFT +3.63% benefited from perceived software capture of AI spend.
AAOI -5.53%, LITE -2.83%, COHR -2.45%, LASR -6.22%, and IPGP -3.16% showed weakness across optics and industrial photonics tied to AI-capex proxy de-risking.
MSFT +3.63% delivered the best relative move among large internet/platform names.
TTD +3.08%, SNAP +2.89%, and PINS +1.83% outperformed on ad-tech and ad-supported engagement strength.
GOOGL -1.15%, AMZN -1.64%, and RBLX -1.91% lagged as broader internet beta traded softer.
META -0.21% and NFLX -0.52% were relatively resilient but still closed modestly red.
CSCO +2.08% outperformed on a weak day as investors focused on its software/security mix and AI networking commentary.
AVGO -3.07% and MRVL -1.65% weakened as AI networking and custom silicon names were de-risked.
NOK -3.55% and ERIC -1.58% extended weakness in traditional telecom gear.
In wireless and RF, HIMX -6.34% and INDI -3.67% sold off, while SWKS +3.64%, QRVO +2.50%, QCOM +2.03%, and MXL +5.19% outperformed late in the session.
AKAM -3.34% declined amid ongoing edge/security transition concerns.
NET -1.45% pulled back but was more resilient than AKAM.
AMBQ +1.12% showed relative strength as an EdgeAI software name.
AI compute flow included NVDA -3.60%, AMD -4.45%, ARM -6.19%, and ALAB +2.75% as one of the few green AI-adjacent names.
POET -15.19% led speculative optical unwinding with no fresh press release or filing in the prior 24–48 hours.
AXTI +12.33% rallied on compound-substrate strength tied to AI optics and RF bottlenecks, alongside a large equity raise.

AXTI priced an 8.56M share stock offering at $64.25 per share for roughly $550M in gross proceeds, up to about $632.5M including the underwriter option, with proceeds earmarked for Beijing Tongmei capacity and R&D.
Commentary framed AXTI as an AI demand and indium phosphide backlog story, with backlog above $100M and Q2 EPS guidance of $0.06–$0.08.
AAOI -5.53%, LITE -2.83%, LASR -6.22%, and IPGP -3.16% showed broad weakness across optical hardware, lasers, and industrial photonics.
ASML -5.17%, KLAC -4.54%, and LRCX -4.45% were sold aggressively as chip equipment names de-rated.
AMAT -0.52% was relatively resilient compared with other tool makers.
ACMR +1.02% bucked the weak tool tape as a China-domestic WFE beneficiary.
Subsystem suppliers UCTT -0.92% and ICHR -1.45% moved lower in line with the tool complex.
Foundry-related names were weak overall, with SITM -5.85%, GFS -3.68%, ASX -3.46%, and TSM -3.11%, while UMC +0.03% remained slightly positive.
Analog and mixed-signal names broadly de-rated, with AOSL -5.43% the sharpest loser.
STM -4.45%, ALGM -4.36%, ON -4.13%, MPWR -3.16%, and VSH -3.15% reflected widespread weakness across analog and power.
TXN -1.70% and ADI -2.05% were weaker but fared better than many smaller analog peers, while NXPI -0.27% held up relatively well on auto content exposure.
POWI +1.58% was the lone notable green analog/power name.
In power and wide-bandgap, WOLF -10.93%, AEHR -5.43%, NVTS -4.02%, and SMTC -1.59% all traded lower on SiC and GaN de-risking.
Pre-market commentary highlighted early strength in PANW +5%, COIN +3.7%, NVDA +2%, and AVGO +2%, but semiconductor strength reversed by the close.
MarketPulse flagged INTC and MU lower pre-market on foundry and memory concerns, consistent with weak closes.
For AMD, NVDA, ARM, and AVGO, probes cited valuation and export-control chatter as overhangs, though no new company filings or earnings catalysts were identified.
CSCO’s relative strength was linked to its software/security mix and AI networking commentary.

callouts.summary: +12.33% on AI-datacenter and compound-semiconductor enthusiasm, supported by a $550M equity offering at $64.25 per share with potential proceeds up to $632.5M and messaging around indium phosphide and backlog.
-10.93% driven by SiC/EV de-risking and ongoing balance-sheet concerns despite no new filings.
-6.83% as HBM and memory positions were cut in a broad de-risking move without company-specific news.
-9.42% on micro-cap AI and memory-adjacent de-risking in thin liquidity, with no new company catalyst.
-9.02% on profit-taking and de-risking in speculative MRAM exposure.
-15.19% amid a speculative optical unwind with no fresh press release or filing in the prior 24–48 hours.
-6.62% as process-control and metrology names de-rated in the broader tools selloff.
-6.19% on concerns around valuation and export controls affecting its AI/CPU architecture story.
-5.71% as foundry skepticism and related commentary weighed on shares.
-4.45% on AI GPU profit-taking and crowded positioning after a strong run.
-3.60% despite earlier pre-market strength, reflecting positioning and macro-sensitive de-risking in AI compute.
-3.07% after pre-market gains faded, as investors de-risked AI infrastructure and custom silicon exposure.
+2.03% on an intraday reversal from pre-market weakness tied to China concerns, highlighting relative strength in mobile and RF.
+3.63% as investors favored its software and AI monetization over hardware beta in a weak tech tape.
+7.39% on software leadership and an earnings/guide-style rerating in collaboration and developer tools.
+2.08% supported by interest in its software/security mix and AI networking positioning.
-5.43% alongside other SiC power names as investors de-risked wide-bandgap and test exposure.
-4.02% on GaN and power semiconductor de-rating within the broader power complex.
-5.17% as part of a broad pullback in chip equipment and lithography tied to capex sensitivity.
-4.54% amid weakness in process-control tools within the chip equipment complex.
-4.45% in a sharp selloff across wafer fab equipment and capex-sensitive names.
+3.08% on ad-tech strength among ad-supported platforms.
+2.89% as ad-supported social media names benefited from ad-tech strength.
+1.83% on constructive ad-tech and engagement trends.

watch_tomorrow.text: AXTI — watch whether Friday’s squeeze follows through after the $550M offering pricing and AI-optics/substrate headlines.
NVDA, AMD, AVGO, MU — monitor whether Friday’s semiconductor selloff in AI compute and memory extends or mean-reverts.
WOLF, AEHR, NVTS — look for any continuation in power and wide-bandgap de-risking after sharp weakness.
TEAM, NOW, CRM, MSFT — track whether software leadership and AI/software monetization themes continue.
QCOM, SWKS, QRVO, MXL — see if late-day RF and connectivity strength persists after sector-wide selling.
INTC — any weekend headlines on foundry strategy, customer wins, or capex could drive outsized moves.
SOXX, IGV, QQQ — monitor whether the software-vs-semi rotation persists into the next session and sets the tone for tech and growth.
